data.title: Cencora to buy EyeSouth Partners’ retina business for $1.1B
data.description:
Medical distributor Cencora (COR) has signed an agreement to acquire EyeSouth Partners’ retina business for $1.1B through its eye care subsidiary, Retina Consultants of America, marking its latest deal-making activity.

In a statement on Monday, the company said that once the transaction is complete, the retina physicians of EyeSouth will join Retina Consultants of America.

Cencora (COR) acquired Retina Consultants of America in 2024 from private ​equity firm Webster Equity Partners for $4.6B.

The company expects the transaction, adjusted for financing costs, to slightly boost its adjusted diluted EPS in the first twelve months after the deal closure. However, Cencora (COR) said its reaffirmed outlook for fiscal 2026 doesn’t expect the deal to close within this fiscal year.

The Conshohocken, Pennsylvania-based company has stepped up its M&A activity in recent months. The EyeSouth deal comes weeks after the company announced plans to divest its MWI Animal Health unit through a merger with privately ​held veterinary product distributor Covetrus as part of an agreement worth $3.5B, including debt.

data.title: everplay group plc Non-GAAP EPS of 25.70p, revenue of £166M
data.description:
• everplay group plc press release (TSVNF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of 25.70p.
  • Revenue of £166M (-0.4% Y/Y).
  • Year-end cash balance of £51.9m.
  • The board has declared a final ordinary dividend of 1.9 pence per share which, including the 1.0 pence interim dividend, takes the total dividend for FY2025 to 2.9 pence per share (FY 2024: 2.7 pence).

data.title: Smithfield Foods rallies after seeing growth across all segments in Q4
data.description:
Smithfield Foods (SFD) rallied in early trading on Tuesday after sliding past consensus estimates with its fourth-quarter earnings report. 

Revenue was up 7.1% year over year to $4.2B for the quarter that ended on December 28. Sales were up in the packaged meats (+4.3%), fresh pork (+2.1%), and hog production (+3.3%) segments.

Adjusted operating profit was up 27.8% year over year to $402M on an adjusted operating margin of 9.5%. More than 75% of the adjusted profit was from the packaged meats segment, and it was the fourth consecutive year of operating profit of above $1B for that segment.

At the end of the quarter, Smithfield Foods (SFD) had $3.8B of available liquidity consisting of $1.54B in cash and cash equivalents and $2.3B of availability under our committed credit facilities. Smithfield Foods (SFD) ended the year with a ratio of net debt to adjusted EBITDA from continuing operations on a trailing twelve-month basis of 0.3X.

CEO outlook: "Looking ahead to 2026, our objective is to again grow sales and profitability, and we see a long runway ahead for future growth led by our flagship Packaged Meats segment and iconic brand portfolio. Our culture of continuous improvement enables us to drive efficiencies and continuously unlock value across our business. We are investing capital in a disciplined manner to support our growth strategies, generate attractive returns, and build sustainable long-term value for our shareholders."

On the guidance front, Smithfield Foods (SFD) expects total company sales to be up low-single-digits compared to FY25. Total company adjusted operating profit of between $1.325B and $1.475B is expected.

Shares of Smithfield Foods (SFD) rallied 7.3% in premarket trading to $25.20. Smithfield Foods (SFD) priced its IPO at $20 per share more than a year ago.

data.title: Xiaomi reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Xiaomi press release (XIACF): Q4 Revenue of RMB 116.92B (+7.3% Y/Y) and the 5th consecutive quarter of revenue exceeding RMB100.0 billion.
  • In 2025, our smartphone shipments reached 165.2 million units.
  • In December 2025, our global monthly active users (“MAU”)2 reached another record high of 754.1 million, up 7.4% year-over-year.
  • As of December 31, 2025, the number of connected IoT devices on our AIoT platform (excluding smartphones, tablets and laptops) reached 1,079.2 million, up 19.3% year-over-year. In 2025, we achieved total deliveries of 411,082 vehicles.
  • "We expect that beginning in 2026, our cumulative R&D expenses over the next five years (2026–2030) will exceed RMB200 billion."

data.title: Fiverr creates an AI video hub to help break the Hollywood production model
data.description:
Fiverr International Ltd. (FVRR) announced on Tuesday that it has launched an AI Video Hub that connects brands directly with a curated group of top independent AI video directors to create cinema-quality commercials, social content, and brand films without traditional Hollywood-style production infrastructure. The move by the Israel-based company was in response to rising demand, with searches for AI video creation on Fiverr up 66% in the second half of 2025, and reflects a broader shift from large, expensive production crews toward solo creators and small teams who can deliver comparable work faster and at a lower cost.

As part of a promotional campaign, Fiverr (FVRR) installed a 30-foot-high hillside sign overlooking the 101 in Los Angeles featuring AI director Billy Boman’s name, symbolically positioning AI creators as the future of brand storytelling. The hub’s roster includes Boman, who has worked with Google, Universal Music Group, YouTube, and Klarna, along with The Dor Brothers (creators of Snoop Dogg’s first AI music video), Julien Nicaud (Dave & Buster’s global campaign), Too Short for Modeling (Dollar Shave Club and Fiverr’s Garry AI campaign), and Jagger Waters, a CapCut creator partner recognized by Adobe, Cannes, and SXSW. 

"For decades, brand video has been at the mercy of the Hollywood production playbook: big crews, big agencies, big budgets, and months of lead time," stated Fiverr (FVRR) CMO Matti Yahav. "That model is breaking. The directors in this hub are producing work that stands next to anything coming out of a traditional studio, and they're doing it faster, leaner, and for a fraction of the cost," added Yahav. 

Shares of Fiverr (FVRR) are down more than 48% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Vertical Aerospace GAAP EPS of -£0.57
data.description:
• Vertical Aerospace press release (EVTL): Q4 GAAP EPS of -£0.57.
  • Looking ahead, Vertical expects net cash outflows of approximately $195 million (£145 million) over the next 12 months, reflecting increased investment in flight testing, certification and manufacturing.
  • As of December 31, 2025, the Company held approximately $93 million (£69 million) in cash and cash equivalents.
  • As of the date of this report, Vertical’s short-term liquidity includes cash on hand (approximately $58 million / £43 million) and anticipated near-term receipts from R&D tax reliefs (approximately $23 million / £17 million) and government grants & VAT (approximately $5 million / £4 million).

data.title: Nevada King Gold secures C$16M private placement; Centerra to take 9.9% stake
data.description:
Nevada King Gold (NKGFF) intends to complete a non-brokered private placement of common shares of the miner, which is fully subscribed, for total gross proceeds of approximately C$16 million, it said on Tuesday.

In connection with the financing, the company has entered into a subscription agreement with Centerra Gold pursuant to which Centerra (CGAU) will acquire 9.9% of the issued and outstanding common shares at a price of C$0.21 per share, matching the 20-day VWAP of the common shares on the TSX Venture Exchange, for anticipated gross proceeds of approximately C$10 million.

Both the chairman of Nevada King, Collin Kettell, and the largest shareholder, Michael Parker, intend to participate in the financing to maintain their pro-rata ownership positions in the common shares, for anticipated gross proceeds of about C$6 million.

data.title: UK flash PMI shows manufacturing resilience, services weakness in March
data.description:
• The S&P Global UK Manufacturing PMI eased to 51.4 in March from 51.7 in February, compared to expectations of a drop to 50.1, preliminary estimates showed on Tuesday.
  • This marked the slowest pace of expansion in three months, as output growth slowed and goods producers cited the war in the Middle East as weighing on global demand.
  • The S&P Global UK Services PMI fell to 51.2 in March from 53.9 in the previous month, firmly below market expectations that it would ease to 53 to mark the softest pace of expansion since September of last year, according to a flash estimate.
  • The S&P Global UK Composite PMI fell to 51.0 in March 2026, down from 53.7 in February and below expectations of 52.9, according to preliminary data. This marks the slowest output growth since September 2025, as both services and manufacturing activity weakened. New business inflows declined for the first time in four months, with firms attributing the drop to falling confidence amid the Middle East conflict, rising inflation, and expectations of higher borrowing costs.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB)
  • Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Siemens, Alibaba team up for industrial AI; Joe Tsai discusses agentic AI market
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) and Siemens (SIEGY) (SMAWF) are expanding their collaboration to make advanced simulation and industrial AI accessible.

Siemens and Alibaba Cloud said they are working to provide computer-aided engineering, or CAE, capabilities as Infrastructure-as-a-Service, or IaaS, for customers in China by combining Siemens' simulation portfolio with Alibaba Cloud's advanced computing and trusted cloud infrastructure.

Under the collaboration, Siemens plans to deploy its cloud-enabled CAE solutions on Alibaba Cloud, enabling customers to access scalable simulation environments, including virtual simulation appliances and high-performance computing clusters, delivered via the cloud.

The companies said that this will help engineering teams run complex simulations more efficiently.

The companies are also exploring how Alibaba's Qwen large language models, or LLMs, could support AI-driven capabilities in Siemens' product lifecycle management software, opening new possibilities for AI-assisted engineering workflows and intelligent product development.

Siemens technologies are also powering large-scale data centers such as Alibaba's Zhangbei Data Center.

Separately, speaking at the Siemens RXD Summit in Beijing, Joe Tsai, chairman of Alibaba Group, talked about the "agentic era." 

"Agents are 'virtual knowledge workers' that can perform many tasks people do today. When you consider the global economy is $110 trillion, with nearly $50 trillion tied to white-collar knowledge workers, you begin to see the scale of market potential: agents can disrupt or enhance a total addressable market ((TAM)) bigger than all other industries combined," said Tsai.

Alibaba's Qwen App had 300M monthly active users as of February. The company noted that it is also elevating Model-as-a-Service, or MaaS, as an organizational priority. 

On Tuesday, Alibaba's research arm Damo Academy unveiled XuanTie C950, a RISC-V-based central processor unit optimized for cloud computing.

data.title: 17 Education & Technology names Sishi Zhou as CFO
data.description:
• 17 Education & Technology Group (YQ) has appointed Sishi Zhou as chief financial officer, effective immediately.
  • Zhou joined the company in December 2020 and was appointed as its acting CFO in June 2025. Prior to joining the company, Zhou held multiple advisory positions in strategic finance at Shell plc (China), and served as senior finance manager in multiple organizations as well as senior auditor at PwC Zhong Tian CPAs LLP.
  • Source: Press Release
